spaces.title: Liquid banking on Hyperliquid: spending crypto without selling it
spaces.description: How HyperBeat built non-custodial spending, credit, and trading in one account across eight currencies.
spaces.quote: Rich people don't sell their assets they just borrow against it—now easily accessible to normal people

spaces.title: Hypurr Hour
Ex-Binance builders launch native mobile app for Hyperliquid trading
spaces.description: Dexari brings seamless perpetuals and spot trading to iOS and Android with account abstraction, multi-chain deposits, and integrated staking.
spaces.quote: Hyperliquid is the first time I've seen a product that rivals what a centralized exchange can do in terms of performance, liquidity, and user experience.

spaces.title: Cosmos collapse, HyperLiquid drama, and why execution beats announcements
spaces.description: Cosmos extracted its users. HyperBeat-Kinetiq tension. Why the best teams stay silent and ship.
spaces.quote: If they're not going to build something good enough on Hyper EVM, we're just going to build it better on Hyper Core. And that'll be that.

spaces.title: Euler on Hyperliquid: flexible lending, Pokemon indexes, and trading terminals
spaces.description: HyperFi brings Euler's modular lending stack to Hyperliquid, enabling complex assets, earn vaults, and a Visa card that lets you borrow against your positions in real time.
spaces._transcript: GM, GM, just going to get the speakers on in a bit, and then we'll begin shortly. Audio test. You're good. I got you, man. You got me. I got you, but I think, okay, yeah, I thought Andrew was a bit off just now, but it's fine now. Yeah, check, check. We are good. Let's share some stuff. Awesome. I think we can wait another minute before we begin shortly. So, we're going to burn. Burn, baby, burn. Has anyone voted no? Do we know that yet? I haven't seen anything. I believe Loracle is one of them, because I think I saw some tweet whereby he was against it. And he said, like, the holders basically should write the ups and downs of the protocol together. So, he was against it. All right. I think we probably can just begin. So, yeah, I think today we have two distinguished guests from Hyper -5, Andrew and Andy. I'm sure many of you guys know Hyper -5 recently just announced yesterday. They had an integration with Euler, which we'll speak, which we'll talk more. More about besides that integration, they have a lot more upcoming works in the next few months. I believe they are rolling out their Hyper -5 card. They have some plans for HIP3 markets and also something related to Pokemon cards. So, I hope Andrew and Andy will unpack all of that in a bit. Yeah, for someone who doesn't know what Hyper -5 is, why don't we start from there? Besides just a lending market, what can users do on Hyper -5? Amazing. I'm going to hand it over to Andrew. So, I'm Andy, head of growth, and I'm one of the main yappers that you guys will see on the timelines. But Andrew is the co -founder, CEO, CTO, and long -time Hyperliquid dev secret weapon, not only helping our team get off the ground in the early days and really become something amazing, but many other teams. And so, yeah. A lot of that has been through our relationships that we've built in the Hyper -Co community. So, thank you for hosting us today. It's great to be here and a chance to talk to all of you in the audience. I see Chroma and Gollum and Imp and Ace and a bunch of the other gangs, Gluex. Thank you guys for showing up, our community, and I hope we're reaching some people who are curious about Euler and us. But Andrew, I'll let you take it away about what we do, who we are, and all that stuff. Cool. Yeah. Thanks for that. Hi, everybody. I'm Andrew. I go online by Andrew Lloyd, colloquially the cat gif of our ecosystem. Yeah. So, what we are is a great question. And I like to always say, what are we today versus what do we want to be, right? And so, for us, it's never been about just lending. Lending is a vehicle and an apparatus to a broader financial ecosystem, not just for ourselves, but for everybody. And we think about it that lending is a commodity. In a lot of respects, right? There are protocols out there that you can boot up and you can turn on. But if you look at our pooled market as a great example there, these are things that are, I shouldn't say solved problems, because a lot of work goes into operating them. But the problem set is relatively established. But if you look at Hyperliquid as an example, and traders more specifically, their needs go way beyond just a simple pooled lending. They don't need necessarily just lending. They need lending against some things. And some things may not be applicable for the pool model. And so, what we are is really thinking about a finance platform for traders in general. So, traders want to speculate. They want to participate in the open markets. They want to save money. They want to spend money. They want to facilitate different opportunities and options. And so, we think about it more broadly and say that we are a lending platform or a financial services platform for traders. And that's what we are evolving more broadly into. We have a full trading terminal that's coming out fully integrated from Hyperliquid first all the way down. We've integrated options into it. It's credit card integrated as well that lets you tap into your positions across all these venues and spend in real time. So, our mission is really to enable you as a trader or someone who is in the markets to have access to A, the markets, and B, kind of like your off -chain real life. And so, I like unintentionally call it a trading platform. I coined what I thought was a really great phrase around what it is that we aim to enable is converting your on -chain positions into a battery that allows you to run your life. And so, facilitating and helping you create products or portfolios that generate revenue or allow you to express your intentions in terms of a trade or a position, but have access to utilize that in real life. A lot of this is born from my experience of being DeFi native for the better part of a decade. And if you think through that, you'll see that there are a lot of different ways to do that. There's the notion of the simplest thing from off -ramping to buy a coffee. In the early days, if you wanted to spend crypto on anything or spend against your assets against anything, you had to just off -ramp large chunks, pay the fees, wait days and days and days to do it. And it really just kills the notion of you having money, right? Because all the friction that is built up and born around the idea of these positions, so much time, energy, and mental energy into maintenance goes around having these positions. And then it sucks when you're like, oh, yeah, real life is calling. I got to go spend some of my resources and do this. And it shouldn't feel bad to spend your money. And I think that's a big part of the equation for me over the last few years is we have this tendency to rather go to zero and bag -hold than it is to actualize any wealth. And I think that's a broken mental framing for a lot of people. But it comes with this conviction that we have and this love for the ecosystem. And I think that's one of the reasons that we're a part of. And so there had to be some kind of design space that made sense to play. And I think we really kind of nailed it in terms of how we see the broader aspect of this evolving. And it goes beyond just being a lending product in a trading execution terminal. But we imagine there is going to be somebody that is going to be like an AI helping you and guiding you and making financial decisions. Not necessarily acting for you, but trying to educate you and not be your friend, but be your mentor and your guide through all the craziness that is out there in DeFi. Getting good, clean understanding around what protocols to use and what investments to make is where most of the value is in crypto, is being able to understand the asymmetric realities that you're dealing with. And so our goal is really to just build a platform over time that gives you, the traders, more tools that you would need across a multitude of venues. And I'm sure a lot of you out there, you don't just use one product or service. If you use HyperFi, you probably use HyperLend. You probably use DeFi. You probably use Felix. You probably use something else for lending. Same goes for trading. And hopefully, I imagine it plays the same way for options. And so we just want to expose our frame and our view of how things should look and feel to you and try to service a broader aspect of your financial needs. That's a long -winded saying of DeFi for traders. Yeah, DeFi for traders. So I got into crypto. If you guys don't know, I'm a former academic. I was a professor. And I researched video games and other new technologies. And I was a brokie. And I'm still kind of a brokie, but even more of a brokie. But I knew stuff. I could predict things. And I could make calls on what social media companies were going to do well. And I could see my predictions coming right. But I had no access to capital and no way to express that. And so in 2017, when I found Ethereum, well, Bitcoin first and then Ethereum, it was like, oh, okay, I can actually participate in these, make my own predictions. And essentially, that was a trade. And I didn't really think of it as a trader until more recently, the last couple years. But I'm trading on information that I had. And I wanted to express a position. And sometimes I'm wrong. But honestly, I've been right more times than not. And so I had a way to get involved in that. And when I look at markets now and I look at Hyperliquid in particular and DeFi in particular, it is still very token heavy and finance heavy. But one of my... The things that I've noticed that are coming up, like prediction markets are coming up and even SocialFi, as much of a dumpster fire as that's been. It's like there are these other sort of positions that you can express in financial terms and benefit yourself and grow your wealth and grow your capital in these ways. But I haven't yet been able to see that in the model of DeFi, in this pooled model of DeFi. And so when I kind of look at Euler and what we're bringing, and it's not just an integration, by the way. We are bringing the Euler stack and we are operating it. We have a great partnership with them. But it is our baby on Hyperliquid. Does that enable some of these weirder financial products that are not quite yet defined? Does that enable us to be able to bring those to markets yet, Andrew? Or in the future, maybe? Yeah. It all comes down to the asset and the liquidity and a little bit of risk. But ultimately, yeah, that's the big flexible unlock we have here. We've got a tool. We've got a toolkit now in a frame that we can look at how we can compartmentalize different aspects of these nuanced markets. Use RWAs as a great example. They may have complex requirements, bonds, whatever it may be, around how they can be utilized. And prior to Euler, there just wasn't a great framing of how you could compose different aspects. And it's a great way to try and frame it for those out there. Anytime that you modify a smart contract, you increase the risk to the smart contract. And so if you think about all the stuff that you may have to do for these one -offs or two -offs of these different assets just to make them work, it's like square peg, round hole problem. With enough beating of the hammer, it'll go through. But it won't be the same thing coming out the other side. And that's what I think a lot of what we have to deal with in a lot of the technologies of the day is. They were also built over the last decade, but they weren't built for the next decade or so. They were built for the next decade or so. And so what's cool about this opportunity here is that we have the tooling and the flexible capacity now to adapt aspects of these vaulted structures and markets to better suit, A, the assets that are coming in, but B, also the environment they're going into. So where some things may not have been possible before, liquidating directly to HyperCore as an example, maybe there is a much better path to go forward to do that now. A little bit of alpha leak for some people. But it goes the other direction, too. Now is that we can, you know, we can curate our markets more effectively, which means we can have more diversity in the types of offerings that can go on top. So like what an asset of assets that have been pooled that are just like fruit in the I don't know. Not that everybody we're bringing anytime soon. Don't make the old model. It makes sense. And more ice that need more. Yeah. That's my audio that character a little bit. But I think I you want to know about some of the assets that could come that wouldn't quite qualify for a pooled model. Cool. Yeah. So, you know, one of those could be vaulted structures, you know, so you can imagine having these, you know, HLP wrappers or index tech wrappers of different different underlying yield bearing positions. These things aren't good for pooled because the risk on them is too hard to underwrite as a pooled model. And that's something to it all comes down to risk. Ultimately, at the end of the day and how you can segment that. So a pool is everybody in the pool basically shelters and shares the same exposure risk of the pool. So if one asset in the pool becomes toxic, then everybody will rush to the non -toxic assets to get out of the pool. And, you know, and so if you think about like a vault that's exposed to multiple money markets, maybe multiple protocols, you know, these things become really hard to underwrite because your risk is no longer contained. It is now spread between not just your protocol. And any of the lenders that you have inside of it, but maybe to the HLP, maybe to hyperliquid, you know, hyperliquids London are earned. They're going to call it. Sorry. The BLP is we were colloquializing it beforehand. You know, these structured products are a great example of things that will will print and do really well on the oiler stack that would never be possible in the pooled model. Really? It's just there's too much like fifth and sixth order effects. You have to do. You have to deal with when it comes to assets that are composed. So what this opens up ultimately, though, is like, you know, a vehicle where highly composed money markets or structures or funds can be isolated into a single asset. And so you can build indexes as a great example, and you could be able to borrow against that index. And then depending on what the index is, you know, the vault could have custom liquidation logic that knows around how to deal with the index and split it up and maybe auction it off. And so it really comes down to, you know, like, not just necessarily asset selection going into the pool, but how you can actually deal with and handle the risk that's exposed between these things. And so, you know, one of the knock on effects is we can get way more efficient markets from LTVs, give users better preferences in terms of how liquidations work. You know, liquidations run in a range now. They're not flat like they are on the Aave pooled model. And so we can also expand and explore different offering types. So, you know, for institutions. For institutional participants who can offer better rates, but they need to know who the users are. We could do things like a zero -knowledge KYC check, things that you can't do in the pool. You can segment off into the vault structure. And so with that, though, also comes like, you know, being able to tap into identity as a part of the equation opens up your ability to facilitate and play with a whole other manner of asset classes, too. And so just so that's clear, like, we'll never force you to KYC with us if you don't want to. There's the open markets that are always better. They're always going to be available for you to play with. But those markets that have requirements that are different, like, we can tune them such so that you're actually able to elect into going into these markets. Completely optional. But one of the cool knock -on effects is if you did get our credit card, it does have, like, KYC, but that is, you know, applicable as a part of the proof. In the future, you could just use the fact that you have the KYC as your ability to enter into some markets that, you know, have a light identity requirement. And one of the cool parts there, though, is that, like, you know, we've talked to a number of, you know, lenders and facilitators who can come and they can beat the rates on Hyperliquid by and large, but they need to know who they're lending to. They can't be beholden to unknown parties. And so we just think that the design space becomes much more different in terms of what finance can actually be. You know, if you want to get a loan against an options position or a purpose position, or you want to do a full -on portfolio margin, you want to tokenize your multisig on Hyperliquid. spaces.title: Y2K's $7M rebrand to Fractality: market-neutral yield on Hyperliquid EVM
spaces.description: Insurance protocol pivots to tokenized yield strategy; $1M community raise on Feb 8.

spaces.title: Ambit's HyperDrive: spot lending and tokenized vaults on Hyperliquid
spaces.description: Ambit Labs brings a one-stop DeFi shop to Hyperliquid—lending against yield-bearing collateral, tokenized HLP vaults, and liquid staking.

spaces.title: HyperLiquid's validator cartel problem and Kinetiq's StakeHub solution
spaces.description: Omnia from Kinetiq explains why liquid staking needs autonomous validator selection, not secret kickback deals.
spaces.quote: For us, that doesn't make any sense. We don't give a fuck about working with any validator in particular.

spaces.title: Order books on Tendermint: why Hyperliquid rebuilt DeFi from scratch
spaces.description: Hyperliquid founder explains why they chose to build their own L1 instead of using existing infrastructure for decentralized perpetuals.
spaces.quote: The entire order book actually runs on chain. There's no one computer that if shut down, the whole thing stops.

spaces.title: SuperSexy brings centralized exchange UX to Hyperliquid, $750M volume in one month
spaces.description: Chris from SuperSexy explains building on-chain finance on Hyperliquid with familiar CEX experience, competitive 6bp fees, and Turnkey self-custody.
spaces._transcript: GM, GM, just waiting for Chris from SuperSexy to join us as a speaker, and then we'll begin. Sorry, just having a bit of technical errors will await Chris to be back on. All right, perfect. I think we can hear you now. Chris, can you unmute again and test your mic? Yeah, yes, thanks for your time. All right, perfect. Okay, I think we can go ahead. So for those unfamiliar with Hyperco, we regularly conduct this PMA. Hyperco is founded by three of us, Kirby, Noel, and myself, Velocity. Started Hyperco with the mission to unite different demographics of users, builders, and traders, and pretty much everyone that are excited about Hyperliquid in this ecosystem. We've done this through... a variety of different key initiatives. I think the first one being the most well -known for the largest validator outside of Foundation Notes, together with Nansen. We have, I think, like, 1 .5 billion in AUM on that stake amount. We also do a lot of content creation in the form of weekly recaps, ecosystem guides, and also project guides, which we've collaborated mainly with Uncoiled, like, recently. We've pushed out a couple of pieces in the past two to three weeks of projects building on top of Hyper -EBM. So do check that out if you are interested to dive deeper. We host a lot of in -real -life events to connect different builders and traders in person. So if you've been to one of those, I think most of you would have enjoyed it. So we really do a lot of stuff in this ecosystem, and I think that's not exhaustive list. So I guess we also help with projects, Spotlight, push -trapping of liquidity and users. So today we have one of the one of a very special project that I've met the founder, Chris, in person a few times and only have great things to say about him. So I'm very excited to have to do this one with him. Chris, why don't you give a quick introduction about yourself and your motivation behind building in SuperSexy? Sure. Thanks, Velocity. It was very great meeting you in person as well. You're a great, great, great guy. My background, I've been building in DeFi for the past six, seven years. I started building, started my crypto career building in DeFi. I was in a prediction market back in 2020, I think. At the time, it was a pre -prev era. It was pre -turnkey. There was no Moonpay for onboarding, on -ramping and off -ramping. And so at the time we were, I was spending a lot of my time trying to figure out how do you onboard normies to trade this on -chain speculative stocks in a way that anyone can try. So I know that I can trade without much knowledge of crypto. And so I spend a lot of my time over the past few years just getting into designs, user experience, and also getting out front end applications. Later on, I spent about a year doing MEB. I did solo searching for some time. Not everyone knows I have this kind of past experience, but I did a year of very competitive MEB searching where I got to learn a lot of things about very low level EVM programming. And that's where I kind of gathered a little bit of my knowledge. of somewhat HFT, a little bit of experience there. And then also spent about a year building a sub -custody wallet called Talisman. It was the largest wallet in the Polkadot ecosystem. I also worked pretty closely with Parity Tech to build a multi -sig product for their day -to -day finance operation guys. They don't necessarily know about gas fees, things like that. And I had to, I was kind of forced to figure out how do you build things with these guys that don't necessarily know how to properly approve a transaction on -chain. And so most of my experience in the past have been related to building high performance and user -facing products, consumer products. Bit of a fun fact, I actually got a chance to interview for a front -end role at Hyperliquid Team back in early 2023, we got to the very final stage of the interview and beyond. We went back and forth on email multiple times, but then unfortunately the time zone didn't work out. But I think that's when I got a first look at Hyperliquid in the very early days and began following the projects. And then earlier this year, I joined our funnel in the holdings. I actually met our partners through a Hyperliquid event as well, but got a chance to meet the gentlemen here and they gave me a lot of opportunities to kind of learn and invest alongside them as well as see how we could help out with some of the ecosystem projects in Hyperliquid. And then got to see a lot of things. And one thing we talked about a lot was, everyone talks about Hyperliquid being the on -chain finance, or on -chain By -Pay, but we looked at a lot of projects. We don't actually see anyone actually doing an on -chain finance. And so the motivation for me was actually, why don't we try to do an on -chain finance ourselves? And so, yeah, there you go. Right. So SuperSexy is definitely a name that is pretty attention grabbing. Do you want to share a bit on how you guys arrived at this name? Yeah, I mean, it's very straightforward. We wanted to build something very similar to Centralized Exchanges, because I think Centralized Exchanges invested a lot of resources and talent into building some of the best user experience in crypto in general. And so we think, like, why reinvent the wheel? Why not just build something that someone has really spent so much time and effort trying to master or perfect, right? And we think, actually, Centralized Exchanges have some of the best user experience. And so I thought, like, I think the way to grow something like Hyperliquid from here is you actually want to try to get more of these people that have never gone on -chain to try to trade on -chain. And so we wanted to build something that is very similar to the experience on a Centralized Exchange, but fully on -chain on Hyperliquid, where you subclassify your assets. And so SuperSexy is basically like, I guess it's like, you know, the app feels like a sex, like an on -chain sex type of thing. Yeah. Nice. So for those who are unfamiliar and haven't actually interacted with SuperSexy, how do you build upon the existing infra of Hyperliquid? And what's the plan with SuperSexy? Yeah. I think potentially most of us here have used Hyperliquid, and you know what Hyperliquid is. But in our view, Hyperliquid is a building block for the next generation of application, much like, you know, Aave or Uniswap and so on, where you can build beautiful applications on top of the logics behind. We're trying to build, leverage Hyperliquid as the kind of auto -matching infrastructure to build beautiful on -chain trading experience similar to Centralized Exchanges, without, you know, users having to compromise on security and separsity of the assets. So EVM, you have to get hyper gas, you have to connect your wallets to multiple sites and so on. With SuperSaxxy, we're trying to create this experience where you just buy hype or buy BTC or whatever, you click on earn, for example, and you can deposit your assets into DeFi protocols in one click, right? And in trading, we're trying to create an experience that most Centralized Exchange users that come here wouldn't feel like they are getting a downgraded experience. Instead, they are getting a experience that is at least on par or upgraded. While also getting an upgrade in their secrecy of their assets. So I would say SuperSaxxy is a front -end that does a lot more than what Hyperliquid offers today, trying to match the experience of Centralized Exchanges on Hyperliquid. Well, I think you just touched on one of the key products about users, since you have Hyperland integrator, users can pretty much do everything they want, right? Everything from buying of hype and supplying of hype, or even majors like Bitcoin, ETH, through Unite, and then supplying it on Hyperland, all through SuperSaxxy itself. We have been setting up a massive infrastructure in the backend to sitting in Tokyo right next to some of the best location to place trades on Hyperliquid. We're setting up some of this infrastructure to be able to offer much, much more complex things for Hyperliquid users that Centralized Exchange have always had privilege to. So things like, actually I'm not gonna leak now, but stay tuned, we're gonna announce something tonight, or release a new feature tonight. We're gonna add a lot more things that a Centralized Exchange users that comes to Hyperliquid today would feel like, wait, why do we not have this here that they are used to in a Centralized Exchange? And so we'll be offering much, much more of these features on SuperSaxxy, and also with much, much more integration with Hyper -EVM ecosystem projects that would make a Centralized Exchange users coming here not think that they are getting a downgrade experience. So yeah, stay tuned, we have a new feature coming up tonight. I was still writing the final line of code for this call, and we'll probably be announcing it tonight. Nice. I know you've been teasing in our group for a little bit, so pretty excited to see what you announce, hopefully in a few hours. So since you have this sort of different features and extra utility on top of Hyperliquid, what are some pain points that you hope to elevate for users? Since especially also like beginners and experienced traders have different needs, how can each of these group best benefit and maximize the experience with SuperSaxxy currently? Yeah, I think we have to first define like the audience we're trying to target here. So then we can kind of pinpoint this, what the pain points we're trying to solve for them. One of the main group of users we actually wanna try to target are centralized exchange users that have never gone on chain. And the pain for these users is pretty straightforward. Some of them, they don't even have a MetaMask wallet. They don't know what a Seapraise wallet is, but they trade a lot on centralized exchanges. Some of them, most of them don't know how to bridge, for example. And so we wanna try to solve the entry point problem for some centralized exchange users. Some of these users that have never or rarely goes on chain. We wanna help them, you know, we wanna build the easiest path for them to come and trade on chain and have their day -to -day activities . There's one thing we learned from speaking with a lot of centralized exchange users is that most of them have concerns around their assets being frozen, for example. Like, I mean, let's follow the white whale on that. I think he's like starting like a war with like Mexi or something because his assets got frozen. I think a lot of people, especially the smaller guys, actually faces these kind of issues every day. But because they're much smaller, they don't get to surface this on platforms like this. And so I think, you know, the people we're trying to help them solve is by leveraging hyperliquid and kind of on -chain experience. These users will no longer have to worry about their assets getting frozen by exchanges, while at the same time, using some of the additional tools we're building for them, it would get this exact same or even better trading experience that they are used to on a centralized exchange. Yeah, I think everyone definitely has seen the saga with MEXC by the white whale about how he controlled his funds and is just going pretty crazy over it, but I do agree. I mean, that's the famous saying of not your keys, not your coins. So, it's definitely quite a high barrier to entry, especially if you've never created your own self -custody wallet in this space. I remember when I first started it was such a pain point. And I watched thousands of videos just to familiarize myself with the wallet and that's before even dabbling on Chain. So I think what you guys are doing with SilverSexy, especially all the different integrations and having them in your app itself without someone having to handle all of these is definitely very helpful. So with that, I think it's helpful to touch on what kind of fee structure you guys have right now and also how do you plan to... I mean, because Builder Code actually allows you guys to have a fee on top of HyperLiquid's existing. So with that also, why should users not just use the HyperLiquid frontend itself? Do you want to touch on your fee structure and also how that extra bit is actually worth it on SilverSexy itself? Yeah. Yeah. So our total... Base fee is about six basis points for completely new users. So if you don't have any discounts or you have not traded any volumes for the volume discounts at all, you would be paying a total fee of six basis points, 0 .06 % of fees, including HyperLiquid fee. We intentionally designed the fee this way to... First of all, we're not here to build for... I guess we're not trying to extract from the existing HyperLiquid ecosystem. And so we think by putting a higher fees, it signals our intention to bring in net new liquidity and users outside of HyperLiquid. And second, the fee structure is actually very competitive to even centralized exchanges. So if you look at some of the top, the T1 and T2 exchanges, your average fees for per trading is around six basis points to seven basis points. Binance, I think, is doing 5 .5 basis points, 5 bit as well. And so... By putting it at six basis points, we're actually on par with a lot of centralized exchanges already. And then if you just use... Even if you just use some of the lowest tier invite codes from our users, you can pretty quickly get your fees to a rate that is much, much more competitive than a centralized exchange at 5 .5 or even five basis points. And so, yeah, total fee is six basis points. But if you just use an invite code and trade a little bit, you can pretty much get to a much, much more competitive rate than a centralized exchange very quickly. Spot fee, obviously, it's way more competitive than a centralized exchange that charges 1%, sorry, 0 .1%. Whereas I think we are charging like... I don't think we're charging people to quote on all of the spot buy at all. It's just on selling, it's like nine basis points or something, which is still much more competitive against a centralized exchange. Yup. I think even with the beta code fee, you guys definitely have very, very competitive and one of the lowest still compared to whatever centralized exchanges like Binance and Bybit are offering. With this, I believe SuperSaxi ranks top five in average revenue per user, which is honestly very impressive, considering that you guys, I think, went live less than a month back also. What do you think contributed to this? And... How are you guys targeting the users that... How are you guys targeting... What kind of users... Sorry, what kind of users do you wish to target? And also, you mentioned like, users who are familiar with Saxos, but I believe a lot of the current profile are still hyperliquid users. So what kind of feedback have they given? And also, how do you... What kind of factors do you think contributed to such a high average revenue per user? Yeah. Honestly, this... I think we picked a lot of the... We checked a lot of the boxes way earlier than I expected us to hit, but... Most of our users today, I think, are mostly power users that are trading larger sizes on even centralized exchanges today. As you may know, we haven't done any active marketings yet. We haven't... We don't have things like a points program yet. We don't have... We're not... We're not doing... We're not very active on marketing yet because we're... We're trying to improve the product to a state where it's actually very sticky for users to use before we want to, you know, do any incentives to attract people to try. Because we don't want to... We don't want to inflate the metrics by just having incentives. We want to actually be able to talk to the users that use it every day and can give us constructive feedback on how we can improve the ad. Actually, if you're an active user, you're likely noticing some improvements here and there every day. Literally just today, we just added a new feature where when you're trying to close a limit order, we would show the best price in the pop -up such that you don't have to close the pop -up to check the price and fill in the limit order. You can pretty much just, like, chase the limit order right within the box. These are the kind of very small details that we're putting a lot of attention to to try to make sure we create a very sticky product for the centralized exchange users. And so right now, I think it's mostly, you know, some of the power users that are giving us a lot of feedback and trading it on a day -to -day basis. In the future, there will be more things that we will be doing to get more users on SuperSexy. But right now, I think our focus is to just work with... I guess I've been very much focused on working with some of the very active users on improving the user experience. Even the recent traction, honestly, I don't know. It was just, like, users using it. We haven't done any, like, paid marketing or campaigns. We're not forcing anyone to use it. I think the feedback we've gotten is just that... I mean, we've processed, like, $750 million of volume so far on natural usage. You can't put $750 million through an app if it's a crappy app, right? And so I think it's just users, you know, really liking the product and using it on a daily basis as their daily driver. Yeah, I agree. I think I was probably one of the earlier users of SuperSexy. And it's really quite an honor that you guys approached Hyperco for user testing. And I think I used it pretty extensively in the beginning. I did not use it as often in the past week because I was just mainly trading on Hyperliquid from then on my other trading wallet. But when you guys first began around a month ago, I think what really stuck to me, and I believe I mentioned to you in private or so, it's my most used app in the Hyperliquid ecosystem. And it's really because it offers such a familiar user interface. So that was what got me attracted to Hyperliquid when I first discovered it, I think, two and a half years ago. So it's the same thing that stuck. With me as well for SuperSexy. It's really the familiarity of the user interface. Everything was smooth. There are some missing features, but that's nothing surprising since you literally just built it just a few moments ago. So with that, I think in the past month or so, you've implemented a lot of the different feedback from not just Hyperco, but a lot of your power users as well. So it's becoming quite a super app, not just for trading, but really a one -stop shop. And I think a lot of people are excited about how much more features you can push down. I think with that, do you want to tease a bit about what kind of upcoming features that you have on the pipeline? It might not necessarily be the announcement that you want to make later, but also a bit more hint on what kind of features you have on the pipeline. But do you want to focus on what's on your roadmap in the next month or three months? Yeah. So one thing I've always wanted to... And by the way, I appreciate you testing it out, testing the product out early on and giving us feedback. That was really helpful. A lot of your feedback were extremely good. One thing I really want to achieve is feature priority to centralized exchanges because you don't want to spend a bunch of budgets on marketing and trying to attract a bunch of sex users to come here and be like, this is missing a bunch of things we're used to on a centralized exchange. And so I think our focus is to get SuperSexy to be feature priority to centralized exchange. And that includes... Actually, I'll just say the feature... We're going to add a new feature tonight to allow for a chase limit where if you have an open order, for example, open limit order, you can just press on the chase button and it's going to chase the limit price for you to kind of hit the top of the book. And those would be our focus to create one of the best trading experience on HyperQuickly. And then after that, we'll be doing more GTM things. So yeah, I better not tease too much here. Yeah, we'll be doing more of that later. Nice, yeah. So on the top of the book topic, so HyperLiquid, I think one feature that I use pretty often right now is on HyperLiquid, I mean, there's a keyword function, but every time keyword on HyperLiquid, when you execute that, it's usually taking liquidity. I think that's probably something that you guys can think about doing or probably in the works. It's in the works. It's being tested right now. We have a few of these, some automated trading features that we're going to add, you know, over the next few days or next one or two weeks, because ultimately, we're building an application that deals with real users' money. We understand that, you know, like building new products, you have to ship extremely quickly. But ultimately, we think my principle here is we're building an application that deals with actual users' funds. And so we don't want to just ship fast and not test and have, you know, flaky products that might execute an order that's inaccurate, for example. But I'm very eager to show you the, you know, the things we've built. Especially the one you just brought up. We have that already. We're testing it. We don't want to announce it yet because we don't, we're just testing it to make sure it's reliable. But we have a lot of that kind of things built out. We actually built a pretty sophisticated engine that will allow us to roll out much, much more of these type of advanced features. And now it's just a matter of testing it out and making sure it's reliable. Actually, one thing I forgot to touch on, on some of the things we'll be adding more to improve user experience is more hybrid EVM integration. But with Super Sexy, we're trying to take a much, much more opinionated approach here where we want to craft every single possible path with user experience for our users. You actually need some kind of guidance, guided paths to help users understand what they're actually doing in your app. I think the problem with DeFi today is most users, when they interact with an app, there's sort of a trust assumption with the app. Like when you go on Uniswap, for example, do you actually read the call data that Uniswap is crafting for you in your wallet? No one actually reads that stuff. Most people don't read that. And so I think to offer much, much more secure and also much, much more intuitive user experience, we're going to be doing much, much more opinionated, handcrafted user experience with the Hyper -EVM protocols that we integrate with. A few of those will be coming up recently as well. Nice. On this topic, do you want to hint or share a bit on what kind of integrations you plan, like which DApps do you plan to integrate into SuperSexy? But we are working on integrations with some of these guys. Gotcha. So maybe just one last question with regards to SuperSexy. I think a lot of... users are especially afraid about self -custody, security concerns in the industry. What steps has SuperSexy taken to mitigate this sort of... the risk of exploits and vulnerabilities? Yeah. So... Yes, actually, that's a very good question. We recently integrated with Turnkey to offer enterprise -grade self -custody solution for our users. Basically, what that means is... Sorry, excuse me. What that means is your keys are stored in Turnkey's TE infrastructure that... even if your phone is stolen, for example, today, your keys are not stored. Your keys are not in your phone. It's in your encrypted infrastructure. We are talking to a few auditors as well to try to get a potential audit on SuperSexy. Audits are pretty common for smart contract projects because you have some contracts, you look at it. I spent about half a year doing smart contract audits myself. And so smart contract audits are pretty common in crypto, but kind of consumer app audits is less common in crypto. So we're talking to a few auditors right now to try to find something that makes sense to help audit and pen test SuperSexy. I think ultimately, on one hand, I care deeply and a lot about security. On the other hand, we have a group of... We have users that usually don't really care about these things. Like, I mean, when you go on a centralized exchange, do you ask the centralized exchange, like, hey, how are you storing my encryption key? How do you store my private key? Where's my secret? Most users generally don't do that. They just have more trust assumptions on the app they use. And so our approach here is, you know, on the front end, when users use it, it's just, you know, you put in your email, you sign in, you have your wallet created. You don't see a lot of, you know, what's going on in the background. But in the background, we are now, you know, trying to get an audit. And I don't know if you saw, one thing I really hate with a lot of applications is when you try to click on, like, a guide or, like, learn more button, for example, they just throw you to a documentation site. And then as if anyone actually reads this documentation site. One thing we're doing is we... Some of the guides we have in SuperSexy, we actually have an in -app pop -up type of thing that shows you the guide within the app. And we're doing more of that education, you know, content within the app to explain to users how SuperSexy is using, you know, self -custody wallets to secure their assets. It takes... It will take a little bit of time for those content to come up. You know, we have to get someone to help with, like, the translations, the and so on to make sure the wordings are correct so users aren't misguided. But... Yeah, I think it will require a lot of education for users to understand, you know, what's even a self -custody wallet, what even self -custody means for them. Yeah. I think, to be fair, a lot of people probably don't dive that deep. So I think just the baseline of reassurance, like whatever you mentioned, turnkey and stuff like that, is probably enough for most of them. spaces.title: 70% APR from liquidations: Felix Protocol's stability pool on Hyperliquid
spaces.description: CDP stablecoin with revenue-sharing liquidations. Deposit FEUSD, earn yield plus hype collateral discounts.
spaces.quote: The stability pool depositors get the first right to buy liquidated collateral and receive the 5% penalty clipped from the borrower.

spaces.title: TopDog: multiplayer trading footprints and guild wars on Hyperliquid
spaces.description: Telegram mini app for copying whale trades in real-time, launching guild battles next week with respect points.

spaces.title: 45% win rate pair trading beats directional on Hyperliquid by multiples
spaces.description: Pear Protocol's basket trading on Hyperliquid shows 45% user profitability versus 20% directional trading, with vaults and LLM launching soon.
spaces.quote: 45% of pair traders are profitable versus 20% directional traders on Hyperliquid, multiples higher because users avoid liquidations with hedging.

spaces.title: Catbal's pyramid NFTs, trait trading, and Hyperliquid's resistance to cartels
spaces.description: How a year-old meme became a tiered NFT project with customizable traits, and why the community fights PayPal influence.
spaces.quote: If you want to be a billionaire in crypto it has to come from somewhere, and it feels off because it is a zero-sum game.

spaces.title: First fully on-chain NFT collection on Hyperliquid EVM, minted by pixel artist
spaces.description: Bren launches tiny Hypercats, the first native on-chain NFTs on Hyperliquid, requiring big blocks and HYPE gas token.
spaces.quote: I like the fact that you can deploy a fully on-chain art contract for like 20 cents, maybe even less on base, and you never have to pay IPFS fees.

spaces.title: Outcome's decentralized resolution and unified margin reshape prediction markets on Hyperliquid
spaces.description: Prediction market builders share how data-driven resolution and portfolio margin solve core problems Polymarket and Kalshi miss.

spaces.title: Margin lending with perp positions as collateral, coming to Hyperliquid
spaces.description: Sentiment brings leveraged lending and coin margin to Hyperliquid's L1, enabling 15-20% APY on wrapped stake hype.
spaces.quote: You can now leverage perp positions as collateral, something only sophisticated market makers had access to before.

spaces.title: Six stablecoin teams compete for Hyperliquid's native USDH issuer mandate
spaces.description: Paxos, Frax, Sky, Bastion, Agora, and Native Markets pitch compliance strategies and revenue-sharing models for Hyperliquid's new stablecoin.
spaces._transcript: GM, GM, good morning, good afternoon, good night. First of all, welcome to Stablecoin Roundtable. First off, we want to thank you for making time today for the speakers and listeners. So thank you for joining us. Today we will have Ball and Alex from Pexels Labs, Nassim from Bastion, Sean from Frex, Drake from Agora, Max and Anish from Native Markets, and Rune from Sky to discuss the latest proposals for the USDH Stablecoin. Before we begin today, some quick ground rules. Speaking time will be evenly distributed and we will be following structured rounds. We will also allocate some time for audience questions at the end. So with that being said, let's get started. Okay, each team. We'll have three minutes to introduce yourselves and the project you represent. We will let you know when the time is up. Let's start with Ball. Can you hear me all right? Yes, sir. Hello. Amazing. Thank you so much for having us on. I'm Ball. Great to meet everyone representing the Pexels Labs team. So I'll just share some of our background and why we're really excited to be a part of the USDH proposal, and particularly why we think we're a great fit to work with the ecosystem on this. And so I'll start with a few points. Pexels Labs has a team that is hyperliquid first and aligned, and we have been building on Hyper -EVM since day one with L -Hype, the only team here that has shipped and maintained real tangible products on hyperliquid since day one. And I can pass it to Alex in a second to share more on the background there. But the points on Pexels as well, and some background for those that aren't as familiar with Pexels. Pexels has been a regulated issuer long term. They've been running for longer than anyone in the space. We are the only player here that has a state trust charter through the New York Department of Financial Services and approval to transition to a federal charter via the OCC. This is particularly important because we believe that USDH is going to be a $10 billion plus asset, if not larger. And I really implore the community to look into and understand what some of the newest regulation requires. And particularly the fact that we're not a private company. That only a federally regulated entity can issue an asset beyond $10 billion. This is not something you can just obtain. We started our OCC process over three years ago, right? And so I think this is a really important point to highlight and for people to understand. If compliant global stablecoin is important to the community, Pexels is the only provider in the space that can do this today. But with that said, compliance is everything. That's not our only strength. We're focused on unlocking growth. And I think the unique thing that Pexels is going to bring to the hyperliquid ecosystem is to grow the distribution of hype and USDH beyond the current hyperliquid ecosystem. We don't believe that this proposal is just about USDH replacing USDC. It's about how we can 10x the entire ecosystem. We think there's going to be $50 billion of USDH, not $5 billion. We think hype and the hyper EVM will be available. They use Pexels infrastructure to access. Right. And they are going to turn to Pexels to integrate their next gen of products. And we believe that hyperliquid should be a part of that journey. And so we believe that our experience as a regulated issuer and our experience building in hyperliquid together is the only solution that provides the community an aligned and truly compliant stablecoin that can 10x the entire ecosystem. Sounds good. Thanks for sharing. All right. So maybe up next we have Drake. Hi. Hi. My name is Drake Evans. I'm the CTO and co -founder of Agora. Agora is a stablecoin infrastructure provider. And we provide tooling orchestration for white label stablecoin products. And we also have launched our flagship stablecoin, AUSD. We work very closely with VanEck for the management of our reserves. And our cash custodian is State Street. You know, for us, you know, we believe that the USDH proposal. You know, very similar to Paxos is about more than just compliance or licensure or whatever else. It's about kind of building stablecoins and using stablecoins to grow the ecosystem. I think for us at Agora, we've always believed that stablecoins should be run like a public good as much as possible. Ultimately, what that means is you pass as much of the revenue back to the people who drive value in the monetary network. You know, we've never believed that the core profitability of our business would actually ever have been powered by rent seeking or, you know, directly off of interest income. And so we're super happy to kind of see the shift in the proposal towards significantly higher rev share. I would say, you know, we believe we're the best team to execute on this and to give USDH and the entire hyperliquid community the best source of additional distribution and use. Sounds good. Appreciate the introduction there. Up next, we have Max. Hey, everyone. And thanks to Hyperco for hosting. I'm one of the co -founders of Native Markets. I'm joined by Anish, as well as Adil from the Bridge team. You know, we set out to build out a hyperliquid native stablecoin. It's been a dream of mine for the past year. And what we're really looking to achieve here is not only to bring that stablecoin to market, but to also see it grow. See it through in its ascendancy, actually scaling it on hyperliquid. And that's kind of where our Native Markets comes from, because we do see the relevance of whether it is spot markets, perk markets, EVM markets playing a role in that process. Hyperliquid has been, in my opinion, right? One of the most compelling ecosystems. One of the most exciting ecosystems over the past two years. And that is just now resonating for folks. And it's been really amazing to see all of the providers, you know, really recognizing that right over the past weekend and all the effort that has gone into these proposals. And you know, I think what we're really excited to see here is just. Taking back a lot of the value that's leaking from the ecosystem, playing a deep role in how the ecosystem moves, grows moving forward and, you know, playing a role in hyperliquid's next chapter of growth. So joined Andreessen Horowitz, where I was CISO on the crypto side and have been helping a lot of the ecosystem teams building crypto to be more secure. So really, we work as a white label stablecoin issuer, very neutral. We don't have our own stablecoin. We don't have our own chain. And just like Paxos, we have a New York trust charter, as well as dozens of money transmitter licenses. So we have a lot of licenses across the U .S. and a very strong connectivity, both to the U .S. banking system as well as non U .S. and really excited at the opportunity to not just launch but really grow the USDH stablecoin and the ecosystem around it the way we think about it. And I've been trying to to post on this card. I have no idea why it's not letting me post. I swear I have the rank. I know that everyone here has been. Has been. Or almost everyone has been trying to check their rank and trying to to to get the level to to post. But and that that that was actually quite funny to see. And so the way we think about it is that there has to be as part of the proposals room for room for flexibility. Right. Thank you. The bootstrap of the liquidity is very different. First is Frax USD, which is our programmable digital dollar built to the genius standard backed by short duration U .S. Treasuries leading tokenized funds from BlackRock and SuperState. The second is Fraxel, which is our high performance L1 we built from scratch, proof that we can run core DeFi infrastructure and build out DeFi ecosystems. And we've deployed a number of other systems. We've deployed and supported assets across 20 plus networks. And then the third is FraxNet, and this is the account interaction layer for our stablecoin that allows anyone to mint, redeem and earn rewards. So the latest on our hyperliquid proposal is that based on community feedback, our USDH would be issued standalone, completely separate from Frax USD. We're in talks to form an alliance with a federally regulated U .S. bank issuer prepared to issue USDH if our proposal is selected. We believe in a post -genius world. Compliance is table stakes. The edge we can provide to hyperliquid is in execution, distribution, incentive, security, speed. And that's where FraxNet matters for hyperliquid. FraxNet allows USDH's underlying income to be streamed directly to the targets hyperliquid governance chooses. And splits are enforced by contracts. We'll build out dashboards to make those flows visible. Anyone can audit them. Economically. We've placed our proposal with the best 0 % Frax take rate and 100 % of the underlying income streamed on -chain to your community. No token grants, no equity asks, no rev share and security and reach are built in. We use layer zero for multi -chain transfers across 20 plus chains, but we go beyond even the average layer zero deployment as well because layer zero technology is open source. We actually operate our own Frax validated layer zero instance, which gives us end -to -end control over cross -chain security and messaging. This is just one of many examples of how our offering isn't copy and paste stablecoin as a service. It's an entire system built from first principles and our experience issuing multiple stablecoins and running our own chain with assets across 20 plus chains means we know how to bootstrap usage and liquidity quickly and safely. No other proposer has their own chain. No other proposer operates across more than a handful of DeFi ecosystems. So if the question is who can help hyperliquid keep building like hyperliquid, our answer is to choose. The team that's already done the hard parts on their own, designed a stablecoin operating system, built a chain, scaled across networks and engineered positive sum mechanics that put every dollar to work for users. We'll also bring federal grade issuance together with DeFi native execution and turn the USTH into a flywheel the hyperliquid community controls. Thank you. All right. Appreciate the introduction, Sean. Yeah. Next up we have Rune from Sky. Thank you. And hello everyone. Thanks for listening in on this space and inviting me here. So I am the co -founder of Sky, which is, well, it's possibly the oldest DeFi project. It's the second largest, sorry. It's the fourth largest stablecoin in the world with more than 8 billion USDS in circulation now. And this is, and this stablecoin is backed by more than $13 billion. spaces.title: KittenSwap's 30% airdrop strategy flips HyperEVM's top DEX in three months
spaces.description: From self-funded to #1 TVL: how two DeFi OGs built the DEX ecosystem wanted using concentrated liquidity and community-first tokenomics.
spaces.quote: Letting people get in early allows the community to form and allows people to really get invested in the protocol.

spaces.title: Kinetic's native LST for Hyperliquid: StakeHub's meritocratic validator selection
spaces.description: Omnia and Magnus discuss Kinetic, a liquid staking protocol built exclusively for Hyperliquid, launching mainnet in late April with four audits completed.

spaces.title: HyperLend brings portfolio margin to Hyperliquid EVM with yield-bearing perps
spaces.description: New lending protocol enables collateralized perpetuals, 50-60% looping yields, and tokenized strategies on Hyper EVM.
spaces.quote: We're going to be that banking infrastructure on the EVM side. Think of it as Hyperliquid being Robinhood, and we're Chase.

spaces.title: Hyperliquid's $850M annual buyback: the perp dex that chose users over profits
